pundit.name: Eric Wynalda
pundit.take: **Rafe has outsmarted these amateurs with his chaos strategy, while Mustard Boy and Ghost Pepper flounder in fantasy delusions.** Look, when you draft a team, you should at least ensure they're in the tournament. The fact that The Noisemaker, young Rafe, is sitting in second with a team as unpredictable as Mexico while picking teams that didn't even qualify is pure chaos genius — something these so-called seasoned managers can learn from. Mustard Boy, Zach's over-reliance on Brazil is like playing Russian roulette with a banana; it's not even a gamble. Brazil will obliterate Haiti in Philadelphia, continuing Zach's charmed run in the standings. Meanwhile, Ghost Pepper, Devin, picking the USA and Norway in Tier 2 is like putting ketchup on a gourmet steak—an amateur move. You're all outclassed by a kid who—let's be honest—probably thinks FIFA is just a video game but still managed to edge past everyone else. This is a joke.

pundit_takes.headline: Zach's Switzerland Dominates Bosnia to Maintain Top Spot
pundit_takes.subtitle: Mustard Boy's secret weapon: drafting teams that can actually score.

pundit_takes.headline: Rafe's Mexico Victory Keeps Him Suspiciously Close to First
pundit_takes.subtitle: The Noisemaker outplaying seasoned managers with teams that qualified.

pundit_takes.headline: Devin Hopes USA Can Score Against Unpicked Opponent Australia
pundit_takes.subtitle: Ghost Pepper banking on spicy local support in Seattle.

pundit_takes.headline: Gunner's Morocco Squad Set to Silence Undrafted Scotland
pundit_takes.subtitle: Bubba G's persistence proving futile but entertaining.

rome_analytics_quotes: Zach's surge is an optical illusion; win probability at a measly 19%.
Gunner's France pick is doing heavy lifting, but Senegal's zero points? Embarrassing.
Devin can't buy a win against Rafe, crushed by a 6-point differential.
Rafe's teenage chaos outsmarting the sim; they never saw this wildcard coming.
